summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their final regular meeting of 2022, covering dental insurance renewal, digital signage design, FEMA grant acceptance for fire department PPE, purchase of tactical shields for police, zoning fee schedule updates, and a year-end employee bonus. The board renewed dental insurance with Superior Dental Care at a decreased rate, approved purchasing two tactical shields using ARP funds, updated zoning fees excluding certain BZA residential categories, and approved a $300 one-time bonus for all township employees in recognition of their work during COVID-19 and the July 2022 tornado response.
decisions: Adopted the agenda
Approved payment of bills for November 29 - December 5, 2022
Tabled recognition of a hero to a later date due to illness
Accepted $100 cash donation from Jamie Ramsey for green shifts (fire department)
Accepted FEMA AFG-S COVID-19 supplemental grant of $11,964.58
Increased revenue in fire EMS fund by $11,964.58
Increased appropriations in fire EMS fund by $11,964.58
Approved out-of-town travel for Fire Chief Edward Myers to attend instructors conference in Indianapolis April 24-29, 2023, not to exceed $1,500
Accepted $125 Kroger gift card donation for fire department from anonymous donor
Amended agenda to move dental insurance item up
Renewed dental insurance with Superior Dental Care for 2023 per revised renewal (decrease of $3,034)
Accepted $125 Kroger gift card donation for service department from anonymous donor
Appropriated $14,400 in ARP funds for police equipment
Approved purchase of two tactical shields and cases from Baker Ballistics not to exceed $14,400
Accepted $125 Kroger gift card donation for police department from anonymous donor
Updated zoning fee schedule excluding BZA residential property maintenance appeal, appeals increase, and variance increase
Set annual organization meeting for January 10, 2023 at 7 PM
Set 2023 health care premium split at 12% employee / 88% employer
Accepted 10 tables for community room donated by Goshen CPAAA and Goshen Lions Club
Requested advance in taxes collected by Clermont County Auditor for 2023
Decreased appropriations in road and bridge fund by $237,449 due to OPWC grant not received in 2022
Approved 2022 township revised revenue true-up with overall increase of $380,301.63
Approved one-time $300 bonus for all township employees from department funds payable before end of 2022
Adjourned at 8:10 PM
action_items: Administrator Rose to request larger version of second digital sign design from vendor for comparison
Administrator Rose to share Dan's original sign column photos for reference
Amanda Wickersham / Michaela to distribute link for Superior Dental Care in-network provider search to employees
Harry Holbrook to submit Appalachian Grant application by Friday deadline
Harry Holbrook to continue working on streetscape and Main Street improvement pricing with county and Chad
Administrator Rose to follow up on Brownfield grant email regarding 1826 Main Street
Chad to get new truck ready and order tools
Laura/Fiscal Officer to process $300 employee bonuses before end of 2022
Administrator Rose to purchase chair carts and new chairs for community room after January 1 using $2,000 ARP funds
key_amounts: $11,964.58 FEMA AFG-S COVID-19 supplemental grant for PPE/gloves
$1,500 maximum for Fire Chief travel to instructors conference
$14,400 in ARP funds for two tactical shields and cases from Baker Ballistics
$100 cash donation from Jamie Ramsey to fire department
$125 Kroger gift cards donated anonymously to fire, service, and police departments (3 total)
$3,034 decrease in dental insurance renewal with Superior Dental Care
$237,449 decrease in road and bridge appropriations (OPWC grant delayed to 2023)
$300 one-time bonus for all township employees
12% employee / 88% employer health care premium split for 2023
$2,000 budgeted in ARP funds for community room chairs and carts
$54 million opened by governor for cleanup sites
notable_topics: Digital signage design selection for township building
Dental insurance renewal with Superior Dental Care - in-network vs out-of-network issues
FEMA COVID-19 supplemental grant for fire department PPE
Tactical shield purchase for police officers using ARP funds
Zoning fee schedule update with residential BZA fees excluded from increases
Appalachian Grant application including Linton Road connector and sidewalks
Brownfield grant update - favorable ruling from Judge Brock
OPWC grant delay for Gibson and Thompson Road paving
2023 health care premium split increase to 12% employee
Community room improvements with donated tables
One-time $300 employee bonus for COVID and tornado response
Annual organization meeting set for January 10, 2023
notes: December 20 meeting was canceled, making this the last meeting of 2022. The board entered executive session at 7:58 PM to discuss employee compensation and returned at 8:09 PM. The date correction on Fire Chief's travel was noted (2022 changed to 2023). The township submitted 32 Brownfield properties but none of theirs appeared in the governor's $54 million cleanup site list for Clermont County's 27 sites. The township recently crossed the 50-employee threshold for dental insurance underwriting purposes. A July 6, 2022 tornado that struck Goshen Township was referenced multiple times.
